title: Advanced Footer, Roboto Headings, Hero Theme Settings & Particle Effects

requirements.text: Redesign the Footer component to be advanced, structured, and visually consistent with the rest of the UI (warm neutral tones, teal accents, card-based aesthetic). The footer should include multiple columns: a branding/bio column, a quick navigation links column, a contact/clinic info column, and a social media links column. Add a bottom bar with copyright text and a divider. Match typography, color tokens, and spacing conventions used in Header and section components.

quotes: Make the footer consistent with rest of the UI. Make the footer advanced and structured.

acceptanceCriteria: Footer renders a multi-column layout (branding, navigation, contact, social)
Footer uses the same color palette, spacing, and border/shadow styles as the rest of the UI
A bottom bar with copyright text and a horizontal divider is present
Social media icons are displayed and link correctly
Footer is fully responsive (stacks columns on mobile)

requirements.text: Import the Roboto font from Google Fonts (in index.html and/or index.css) and apply it as the primary font for all headings (h1–h6) and section titles across the entire application, including the Header, Hero section, About, Services, Clinics, SocialMedia pages, and Footer. Update the Tailwind config fontFamily to reference Roboto for the heading/display font stack.

quotes: Use premium font like ROBOTO for all headings.

acceptanceCriteria: Roboto font is loaded from Google Fonts
All h1–h6 elements and explicit heading/title classes use Roboto
Tailwind config reflects Roboto in the font family configuration
No heading in the app falls back to a non-Roboto font when Roboto is available

requirements.text: Expand the HeroSettingsEditor admin component into a powerful, comprehensive hero theme settings panel. It should include: gradient start and end color pickers, overlay opacity slider, text color selector, background blur toggle, glassmorphism intensity slider, hero height selector (options: compact/normal/full-screen), animation speed control, enable/disable mouse parallax toggle, and a live preview thumbnail of the combined settings. Persist all new fields via the updateHeroSettings backend mutation and ensure the Home/Hero page reads and applies all settings.

quotes: Add powerful hero theme settings in admin section

acceptanceCriteria: HeroSettingsEditor displays all new controls: gradient pickers, overlay opacity, text color, blur toggle, glassmorphism slider, height selector, animation speed, mouse parallax toggle
A live preview thumbnail reflects the current settings combination
All settings are persisted to the backend and reloaded on page load
The Hero section on the public page correctly applies all saved theme settings
Controls are grouped logically with clear labels and sections

requirements.text: Implement an advanced particle effect selection system in the HeroSettingsEditor admin panel. Provide a gallery/grid of named particle presets the admin can choose from, including at minimum: None, Floating Dots, Stars, Snow, Bubbles, Geometric Shapes, DNA Helix, Medical Pulse, Confetti, and Fireflies. Each preset should display a small animated preview thumbnail. Additionally, expose fine-tuning controls for the selected preset: particle count slider, particle size range, particle speed slider, particle color picker, and particle opacity slider. Implement the actual particle animations in the ParticleCanvas component on the hero section using canvas-based or CSS animation rendering.

quotes: add advanced particle effect selecion abilities

acceptanceCriteria: Admin particle settings panel shows a grid of at least 10 named presets with animated preview thumbnails
Selected preset name is saved to hero settings in the backend
Fine-tuning controls (count, size, speed, color, opacity) are present and functional
ParticleCanvas component renders the selected particle preset with fine-tuned parameters on the Hero section
Changing preset in admin updates the live hero section after save
'None' preset disables all particles cleanly

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file
Roboto must be loaded from Google Fonts, not bundled locally
Particle effects must be implemented client-side only (canvas or CSS); no backend computation for animations
nonGoals: Changing the overall site structure or navigation beyond footer updates
Adding new backend data models unrelated to hero settings or footer content
Implementing real-time preview sync between admin and public page
Adding new page routes or sections not already present
